steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that the longer a species has been extinct, the less likely a suitable habitat remains, so we need the candidate that has been extinct the longest.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B (saber-toothed cat, extinct 11,000 years ago) gives the candidate species with the longest period of extinction. Choice C incorrectly describes the woolly mammoth as becoming extinct before the saber-toothed cat, but the table shows the mammoth became extinct 6,400 years before present and the saber-toothed cat 11,000 years before present.

The other choices describe species that died out far more recently (passenger pigeon, Caribbean monk seal in 1952).
That is the correct answer.
